programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: , Episode 23, Season 5. Japan is a hot spot for discovering dinosaur fossils. An excavation in Hokkaido sparked a nationwide enthusiasm for dinosaurs, leading to entertainment such as dinosaur robots and mechanical suits. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Vanishing Tools. As the rising age of artisans puts the survival of certain crafts at risk, we shine a light on the vanishing tools used to keep them alive, and their anonymous designs.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: . Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: Take Tech
programme.desc: . A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Japan's Iconic Truth
programme.desc: We explore various truths about sake from the koji that determines the taste to medieval innovations that changed sake-making. We also examine the science behind why sake changes flavor when heated.

programme.title: Hit the Road 5min
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a variety of short programs focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Centuries ago, Tokyo was known as EDO. More than a million people enjoyed life in this city, they live on in ukiyo-e woodblock prints. Take a deep dive into ukiyo-e and explore the soul of old Tokyo.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 8, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 3. Daniel Moore takes a cinematic journey through Nagano. In springtime he hits the south, experiencing the breathtaking cherry blossoms, and in winter gets lost in magical winter landscapes up north.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Mornings begin early for fishmongers and vegetable peddlers at Nihonbashi Bridge, and here is a reason. They are not going to bow to the lord's procession. The bustling scene from everyday Edo life.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters.

programme.title: Design museum Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 8, Season 2. Tagawa Kinya (Design engineer)investigates the process of design by looking at the work of Yanagi Sori, who shaped products by making models using different materials.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.desc: . Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores the sometimes tragic and occasionally comical relationships between married couples in kabuki. Explore these dramatic tales of fidelity and romance.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Isolado Reemerge
programme.desc: . In the heart of the Amazon, the Isolado have reemerged. Astonishing new footage reveals how these uncontacted people have changed over the last decade.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Nhk World Showcase
programme.desc: NHK WORLD SHOWCASE covers a variety of genres, including special features and pilot episodes of newly-developed programs.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Japan's Iconic Truth
programme.desc: We explore various truths about sake from the koji that determines the taste to medieval innovations that changed sake-making. We also examine the science behind why sake changes flavor when heated.

programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a variety of short programs focusing on Japan and Asia.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Centuries ago, Tokyo was known as EDO. More than a million people enjoyed life in this city, they live on in ukiyo-e woodblock prints. Take a deep dive into ukiyo-e and explore the soul of old Tokyo.

programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 8, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 3. Daniel Moore takes a cinematic journey through Nagano. In springtime he hits the south, experiencing the breathtaking cherry blossoms, and in winter gets lost in magical winter landscapes up north.

programme.desc: . Mornings begin early for fishmongers and vegetable peddlers at Nihonbashi Bridge, and here is a reason. They are not going to bow to the lord's procession. The bustling scene from everyday Edo life.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Vanishing Tools. As the rising age of artisans puts the survival of certain crafts at risk, we shine a light on the vanishing tools used to keep them alive, and their anonymous designs.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: . Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: Take Tech
programme.desc: . A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Train Cruise
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 11. Spring arrives late in eastern Hokkaido. Travel from Obihiro through Japan's food base and along the Pacific Ocean to Nemuro. Taste local delicacies, drive a train, and explore the vast marshlands.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: , Episode 23, Season 5. Japan is a hot spot for discovering dinosaur fossils. An excavation in Hokkaido sparked a nationwide enthusiasm for dinosaurs, leading to entertainment such as dinosaur robots and mechanical suits. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.desc: . Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores the sometimes tragic and occasionally comical relationships between married couples in kabuki. Explore these dramatic tales of fidelity and romance.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Nhk World Showcase
programme.desc: NHK WORLD SHOWCASE covers a variety of genres, including special features and pilot episodes of newly-developed programs.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: , Episode 6, Season 2. It's the world's fastest land animal. But that rarely helps in Africa's bush where the cheetah thrives. Let's discover some of its amazing hunting techniques, including what looks like teleporting!. Bill Sullivan, Jeff Manning, Tetsunori Kikuchi, Masahiro Hayakawa, Koichi Okumura.

programme.title: Take Tech
programme.desc: . A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: , Episode 6, Season 2. It's the world's fastest land animal. But that rarely helps in Africa's bush where the cheetah thrives. Let's discover some of its amazing hunting techniques, including what looks like teleporting!. Bill Sullivan, Jeff Manning, Tetsunori Kikuchi, Masahiro Hayakawa, Koichi Okumura.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 2. The Amakusa region has over 120 islands and is home to one of the places where a unique form of Christianity developed. See Amakusa's historic sights and natural beauty through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: . Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: Take Tech
programme.desc: . A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Train Cruise
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 11. Spring arrives late in eastern Hokkaido. Travel from Obihiro through Japan's food base and along the Pacific Ocean to Nemuro. Taste local delicacies, drive a train, and explore the vast marshlands.

programme.desc: , Episode 23, Season 5. Japan is a hot spot for discovering dinosaur fossils. An excavation in Hokkaido sparked a nationwide enthusiasm for dinosaurs, leading to entertainment such as dinosaur robots and mechanical suits. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Japan's Iconic Truth
programme.desc: We explore various truths about sake from the koji that determines the taste to medieval innovations that changed sake-making. We also examine the science behind why sake changes flavor when heated.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 3. Daniel Moore takes a cinematic journey through Nagano. In springtime he hits the south, experiencing the breathtaking cherry blossoms, and in winter gets lost in magical winter landscapes up north.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . Mornings begin early for fishmongers and vegetable peddlers at Nihonbashi Bridge, and here is a reason. They are not going to bow to the lord's procession. The bustling scene from everyday Edo life.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters.

programme.title: Design museum Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 8, Season 2. Tagawa Kinya (Design engineer)investigates the process of design by looking at the work of Yanagi Sori, who shaped products by making models using different materials.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.desc: . Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores the sometimes tragic and occasionally comical relationships between married couples in kabuki. Explore these dramatic tales of fidelity and romance.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Indictment: Survivors of Sexual Violence in Kosovo 25 Years Later
programme.desc: Vasfije Krasniqi Goodman was the first woman to speak publicly about sexual violence during the Kosovo War. This documentary portrays the survivors' struggles and pursuit of justice 25 years later.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. Tokyo's oldest izakaya, with its traditional fare and lucky cats, surviving war and pandemic, has been a place to feel at ease for over 100 years.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Berlin 2026
programme.desc: A look at SHINOMIYA Yoshitoshi's "A NEW DAWN" and other films shown at the Berlin International Film Festival, as well as the classic "A Story from Chikamatsu" by MIZOGUCHI Kenji.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: J-MELO
programme.desc: Join May J. for great Japanese music! This week, anisong legend Takahashi Yoko sings Neon Genesis Evangelion theme songs. Also coming to chat and perform is Ado-produced idol group Phantom Siita.

programme.title: Little Charo's Adventure
programme.desc: Little Charo is an animation series about a Japanese dog who got lost in NY. "I want to see my owner again!" Charo starts his adventure back home. Season 1-5.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 5. Japanese men are increasingly using makeup and hair removal treatment. But historically, makeup was actually the norm. We look at the evolution of male aesthetics in Japan. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 5, Season 3. Todaiji Temple is a World Heritage Site. We encounter cats-and also deer-who live at the temple. One cat keeps an eye on his owner's pickle shop. Another cat enjoys listening to flute music.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan Highlights
programme.desc: , Episode 9, Season 12. The second episode of our Taiwan series features indigenous cultures in the south and on the east coast. We meet a Rukai man preserving the forest and Bunun people with a stunning choral tradition.

programme.title: I Love 'Mee'
programme.desc: Mee wets the bed even after making sure to go to the bathroom and not drink any water before bed. Through a "Bedwetting Counselor," Mee learns that physical growth varies from person to person.

programme.title: Design Ah neo
programme.desc: Design is fun! Design feels good! Cutting-edge images and music guide children to enjoy exploring the ideas and tweaks in the designs all around us. An episode on Entrances.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: . Bari is a port town located at the heel of the Italian peninsula. An important hub of commerce since the time of ancient Rome, it's known for welcoming people of diverse cultures from Asia and Africa. Tony Bill, Dennis Falt, Lynn Harris.

programme.title: The Seasons Of Yamato
programme.desc: . A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons. Koichi Hozan.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 8, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: . Take a drive from Fukui to Kumagawajuku, a historic post town with Edo-period charm, and discover the exquisite craftsmanship of lacquered chopsticks.

programme.title: Core Kyoto Mini
programme.desc: . Kata-yuzen, the stenciled beauty of dyeing. Dive deep into the ancient capital, still innovative 1,200 years after its foundation.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 17, Season 1. Accelerate your Japanese language skills! Learn practical, effective workplace expressions, understand business culture and improve your kanji.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Episode 18, Season 4. A bread maker for many years, Yoshioka Kenichi had a problem with leftover bread crusts. Seeking some use for them, he's put the same love he has for bread into making delicious craft beer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Indictment: Survivors of Sexual Violence in Kosovo 25 Years Later
programme.desc: Vasfije Krasniqi Goodman was the first woman to speak publicly about sexual violence during the Kosovo War. This documentary portrays the survivors' struggles and pursuit of justice 25 years later.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: A look at SHINOMIYA Yoshitoshi's "A NEW DAWN" and other films shown at the Berlin International Film Festival, as well as the classic "A Story from Chikamatsu" by MIZOGUCHI Kenji.

programme.title: The Seasons Of Yamato
programme.desc: . A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons. Koichi Hozan.

programme.desc: . Bari is a port town located at the heel of the Italian peninsula. An important hub of commerce since the time of ancient Rome, it's known for welcoming people of diverse cultures from Asia and Africa. Tony Bill, Dennis Falt, Lynn Harris.

programme.desc: . Little Charo is an animation series about a Japanese dog who got lost in NY. "I want to see my owner again!" Charo starts his adventure back home. Season 1-5. Risa Junna.

programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 5. Japanese men are increasingly using makeup and hair removal treatment. But historically, makeup was actually the norm. programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Episode 18, Season 4. A bread maker for many years, Yoshioka Kenichi had a problem with leftover bread crusts. Seeking some use for them, he's put the same love he has for bread into making delicious craft beer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Indictment: Survivors of Sexual Violence in Kosovo 25 Years Later
programme.desc: Vasfije Krasniqi Goodman was the first woman to speak publicly about sexual violence during the Kosovo War. This documentary portrays the survivors' struggles and pursuit of justice 25 years later.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Berlin 2026
programme.desc: A look at SHINOMIYA Yoshitoshi's "A NEW DAWN" and other films shown at the Berlin International Film Festival, as well as the classic "A Story from Chikamatsu" by MIZOGUCHI Kenji.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan Highlights
programme.desc: , Episode 9, Season 12. The second episode of our Taiwan series features indigenous cultures in the south and on the east coast. We meet a Rukai man preserving the forest and Bunun people with a stunning choral tradition.

programme.title: Design Ah neo
programme.desc: Design is fun! Design feels good! Cutting-edge images and music guide children to enjoy exploring the ideas and tweaks in the designs all around us. An episode on Entrances.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: J-MELO
programme.desc: Join May J. for great Japanese music! This week, anisong legend Takahashi Yoko sings Neon Genesis Evangelion theme songs. Also coming to chat and perform is Ado-produced idol group Phantom Siita.

programme.title: Little Charo
programme.desc: . Little Charo is an animation series about a Japanese dog who got lost in NY. "I want to see my owner again!" Charo starts his adventure back home. Season 1-5. Risa Junna.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 5. Japanese men are increasingly using makeup and hair removal treatment. But historically, makeup was actually the norm. We look at the evolution of male aesthetics in Japan. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 5, Season 3. Todaiji Temple is a World Heritage Site. We encounter cats-and also deer-who live at the temple. One cat keeps an eye on his owner's pickle shop. Another cat enjoys listening to flute music.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: . Bari is a port town located at the heel of the Italian peninsula. An important hub of commerce since the time of ancient Rome, it's known for welcoming people of diverse cultures from Asia and Africa. Tony Bill, Dennis Falt, Lynn Harris.

programme.title: The Seasons Of Yamato
programme.desc: . A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons. Koichi Hozan.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 8, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: . Take a drive from Fukui to Kumagawajuku, a historic post town with Edo-period charm, and discover the exquisite craftsmanship of lacquered chopsticks.

programme.title: Core Kyoto Mini
programme.desc: . Kata-yuzen, the stenciled beauty of dyeing. Dive deep into the ancient capital, still innovative 1,200 years after its foundation.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 17, Season 1. Accelerate your Japanese language skills! Learn practical, effective workplace expressions, understand business culture and improve your kanji.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Episode 18, Season 4. A bread maker for many years, Yoshioka Kenichi had a problem with leftover bread crusts. Seeking some use for them, he's put the same love he has for bread into making delicious craft beer.

programme.title: Indictment: Survivors of Sexual Violence in Kosovo 25 Years Later
programme.desc: Vasfije Krasniqi Goodman was the first woman to speak publicly about sexual violence during the Kosovo War. This documentary portrays the survivors' struggles and pursuit of justice 25 years later.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. Tokyo's oldest izakaya, with its traditional fare and lucky cats, surviving war and pandemic, has been a place to feel at ease for over 100 years.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Berlin 2026
programme.desc: A look at SHINOMIYA Yoshitoshi's "A NEW DAWN" and other films shown at the Berlin International Film Festival, as well as the classic "A Story from Chikamatsu" by MIZOGUCHI Kenji.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan Highlights
programme.desc: , Episode 9, Season 12. The second episode of our Taiwan series features indigenous cultures in the south and on the east coast. We meet a Rukai man preserving the forest and Bunun people with a stunning choral tradition.

programme.title: I Love 'Mee'
programme.desc: Mee wets the bed even after making sure to go to the bathroom and not drink any water before bed. Through a "Bedwetting Counselor," Mee learns that physical growth varies from person to person.

programme.title: Design Ah neo
programme.desc: Design is fun! Design feels good! Cutting-edge images and music guide children to enjoy exploring the ideas and tweaks in the designs all around us. An episode on Entrances.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: Join May J. for great Japanese music! This week, anisong legend Takahashi Yoko sings Neon Genesis Evangelion theme songs. Also coming to chat and perform is Ado-produced idol group Phantom Siita.

programme.title: Little Charo
programme.desc: . Little Charo is an animation series about a Japanese dog who got lost in NY. "I want to see my owner again!" Charo starts his adventure back home. Season 1-5. Risa Junna.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 5. Japanese men are increasingly using makeup and hair removal treatment. But historically, makeup was actually the norm. We look at the evolution of male aesthetics in Japan. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.desc: , Episode 5, Season 3. Todaiji Temple is a World Heritage Site. We encounter cats-and also deer-who live at the temple. One cat keeps an eye on his owner's pickle shop. Another cat enjoys listening to flute music.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: . Take a drive from Fukui to Kumagawajuku, a historic post town with Edo-period charm, and discover the exquisite craftsmanship of lacquered chopsticks.

programme.title: Core Kyoto Mini
programme.desc: . Kata-yuzen, the stenciled beauty of dyeing. Dive deep into the ancient capital, still innovative 1,200 years after its foundation.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 17, Season 1. Accelerate your Japanese language skills! Learn practical, effective workplace expressions, understand business culture and improve your kanji.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Episode 18, Season 4. A bread maker for many years, Yoshioka Kenichi had a problem with leftover bread crusts. Seeking some use for them, he's put the same love he has for bread into making delicious craft beer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: . Bari is a port town located at the heel of the Italian peninsula. An important hub of commerce since the time of ancient Rome, it's known for welcoming people of diverse cultures from Asia and Africa. Tony Bill, Dennis Falt, Lynn Harris.

programme.title: The Seasons Of Yamato
programme.desc: . A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons. Koichi Hozan.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from March 8, Day 1 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Osaka.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: . All moms need some "me time." That's why one forward-thinking woman in Kyoto established Mamo Heart, a space for mothers to not only relax, but also get their own business ideas off the ground.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 1. Global warming leads to widespread famine. By the end of the century, it is predicted that yields of maize, soybeans, and wheat may fall by 30-40%. We examine the scientific data on this global issue.

programme.title: Design museum Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 8, Season 2. Tagawa Kinya (Design engineer)investigates the process of design by looking at the work of Yanagi Sori, who shaped products by making models using different materials.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: . This time, we explore a Nanbu ironware factory blending traditional molds and casting techniques with modern design. See masterful skills and a president's passion for ironware reimagined for today.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: . Shugendo, a unique spiritual tradition, dates back 1,300 years. Through beautiful time-lapse footage, we show its practitioners and Kumano's sacred forests, long associated with death and rebirth.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Seasons Of Yamato
programme.desc: . A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces, makes you experience each seasons. Koichi Hozan.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: , Episode 31, Season 1. The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to bow and arrow. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 19. #19 I'd like a pair of gloves. / Tell store clerks what you want by putting "ga hoshiin desu ga" after the name of the item.

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: . A popular cafe in Osaka offers international dishes on a day-by-day rotating basis. This helps the foreign residents who prepare them, called "one-day chefs," to get involved in Japanese society.

programme.title: Hello! NHK World Japan
programme.desc: . We intorduce the contents and activities of NHK WORLD-JAPAN to make the station and programs known to more people all over the world. This is the window of the station to the world.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 9, Season 1. Sweet-bean-paste-filled taiyaki in the auspicious shape of a seabream or Tai. The Japanese have loved these sweet treats for over 100 years.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . In September, 11 districts in Yatsuo, Toyama Prefecture parade along different routes performing Etchu Owara-Kaze-no-Bon, a 300-year-old matsuri that features elegant music, dancing, and singing.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 11. This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 5, Season 1. In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Episode 11, Season 7. Introducing asari, Japan's most popular clam. Visit Aichi to feast your eyes on asari dishes and see how they're cultivated using suspended cages. How about diving for some giant asari?

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: , Episode 6, Season 2. It's the world's fastest land animal. But that rarely helps in Africa's bush where the cheetah thrives. Let's discover some of its amazing hunting techniques, including what looks like teleporting!. Bill Sullivan, Jeff Manning, Tetsunori Kikuchi, Masahiro Hayakawa, Koichi Okumura.
